rationale: Review 2's most material finding — making general private helpers like `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_id/map_comp` public for downstream TauCeti reuse — is real but contested (private stepping-stones pending Mathlib upstreaming is common practice); its other findings (`includeRight_apply`, specialized simp lemmas, `antipode_tmul` indirection) are borderline or not clearly improvements. Review 1 correctly identifies the main content as genuinely new, missing only the minor private-helper concern. Neither set of actions would leave the merged PR meaningfully better on the reuse rubric.
